channel.title: Sports
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Sports, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Steve Cohen Says Mets Will ‘Never’ Have a Team Captain as Long as He’s Owner
item.description: New York Mets owner Steve Cohen likes the vibe in a revamped clubhouse, and says he’ll never have a captain in charge of that scene.

item.title: MLB Spring Training Buzz: Padres Sign Former Dodgers RHP Walker Buehler
item.description: It's time to dust off those ball bags — MLB spring training has commenced!

item.title: Vladimir Guerrero Jr. Says He’s ‘Turned the Page’ on Blue Jays’ World Series Heartbreak
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays are ready to move on from the 2025 World Series.

item.title: Heisman Winner Fernando Mendoza Says He'd Be 'Blessed And Honored' To Play For Raiders
item.description: Heisman Trophy winner and national champion QB Fernando Mendoza is now starting to really shift his mindset toward the NFL.

item.title: Wrexham Set For Blockbuster FA Cup Tie Against 8-time Champion Chelsea
item.description: Wrexham will host eight-time champion Chelsea for a place in the FA Cup quarterfinals next month.

item.title: USA's Meyers Taylor, 41, wins 1st Olympic gold
item.description: The United States' Elana Meyers Taylor won the women's monobob title Monday to win her first Olympic gold medal at age 41.

item.title: Heise 3 lifts ISU hours after sister scores for U.S.
item.description: Hours after sister Taylor Heise scored to help the U.S. women's Olympic hockey team beat Sweden 5-0 in Milan, Nate Heise hit a go-ahead 3-pointer in N...

item.title: Transfer rumors, news: Man United eye shock move for Liverpool's Mac Allister
item.description: Manchester United are considering a shock move for Liverpool midfielder Alexis Mac Allister. Transfer Talk has the latest.

item.title: Hill, cut by Fins, vows 'the Cheetah will be back'
item.description: The Dolphins released Tyreek Hill in a flurry of roster moves that will clear significant salary cap space.

item.title: Men's AP Top 25 poll reaction: Michigan is the new No. 1
item.description: After nine straight weeks at the top, the Wildcats fall after back-to-back losses, making way for the Wolverines.
